DmWalkDir

Provides a way to examine information about each file in a particular directory. Call DmWalkDir repeatedly to iterate over the files in a directory.

DMHRAPI DmWalkDir(
  PDM_WALK_DIR        *ppWalkDir, 
  LPCSTR              szDir, 
  PDM_FILE_ATTRIBUTES pDmFileAttrib
  );

Parameters

ppWalkDir
Pointer to a structure that DmWalkDir uses internally to iterate over a list of DM_FILE_ATTRIBUTES structures that define the attributes of each file in a given directory. Set ppWalkDir to NULL on the first call to DmWalkDir; the function allocates memory to store a list of files and sets the ppWalkDir pointer to the address of beginning of that list.
szDir
Directory to walk.
pDmFileAttrib
Pointer to a DM_FILE_ATTRIBUTES structure that contains information about the next file in the directory.

Return Values

XBDM_NOERR is returned if the call to DmWalkDir is successful and the end of the list has not been reached. XBDM_ENDOFLIST is returned when the walk is complete. Otherwise, an error value is returned.

Remarks

If the return is not XBDM_NOERR or XBDM_ENDOFLIST, then an error has occurred and the walk should be discontinued. After using DmWalkDir, you must free the memory allocated to store the list of files by calling DmCloseDir, passing it the value stored in the ppWalkDir pointer.

The following example illustrates how to use DmWalkDir and DmCloseDir to iterate over the files in the root directory of the E: drive.

DMHRAPI error;
PDM_WALK_DIR pWalkDir = NULL;
DM_FILE_ATTRIBUTES fileAttr;

do
{
    error = DmWalkDir(&pWalkDir, "E:\\", &fileAttr);

    // Examine the contents of fileAttr here.
}
while (error == XBDM_NOERR);

if (error != XBDM_ENDOFLIST)
{
    // Handle error.
}

DmCloseDir(pWalkDir);

Requirements

Header: Declared in XboxDbg.h
Import Library: Use XboxDbg.lib

See Also

DmCloseDir, DM_FILE_ATTRIBUTES
